# link11waap_backend_service (Resource)

Manages a Backend Service in Link11 WAAP.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Example: Backend Service Resource

data "link11waap_config" "main" {}

# Single-host backend service
resource "link11waap_backend_service" "example" {
  config_id = data.link11waap_config.main.id
  name      = "Example Backend Service"

  # Optional: description
  # description = "Backend service for main website"

  # Whether to use HTTP/1.1 for upstream connections
  http11 = true

  # Transport protocol. Valid values: default, http, https, port_bridge
  transport_mode = "default"

  # Load balancing stickiness. Valid values: none, autocookie, customcookie, iphash, least_conn
  sticky = "none"

  # Whether to use least-connections load balancing
  least_conn = false

  # Optional: custom cookie name when sticky is "customcookie"
  # sticky_cookie_name = "my_cookie"

  # Optional: mTLS certificate for backend connections
  # mtls_certificate        = link11waap_certificate.mtls.id
  # mtls_trusted_certificate = link11waap_certificate.ca.id

  back_hosts {
    host          = "origin.example.com"
    http_ports    = [80]
    https_ports   = [443]
    weight        = 1
    max_fails     = 3
    fail_timeout  = 10
    down          = false
    monitor_state = ""
    backup        = false
  }
}

# Multi-host backend service with failover
resource "link11waap_backend_service" "multi_host" {
  config_id = data.link11waap_config.main.id
  name      = "Multi-Host Backend"

  http11         = true
  transport_mode = "default"
  sticky         = "none"
  least_conn     = false

  back_hosts {
    # host must be resolvable by Link11's infrastructure, e.g. via public DNS or private peering
    host          = "primary.example.com"
    http_ports    = [80]
    https_ports   = [443]
    weight        = 1
    max_fails     = 3
    fail_timeout  = 10
    down          = false
    monitor_state = ""
    backup        = false
  }

  back_hosts {
    # host must be resolvable by Link11's infrastructure, e.g. via public DNS or private peering
    host          = "secondary.example.com"
    http_ports    = [80]
    https_ports   = [443]
    weight        = 1
    max_fails     = 3
    fail_timeout  = 10
    down          = false
    monitor_state = ""
    backup        = true
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `config_id` (String) The configuration ID.
- `http11` (Boolean) Whether to use HTTP/1.1 for upstream connections.
- `least_conn` (Boolean) Whether to use least-connections load balancing.
- `name` (String) The name of the backend service.
- `sticky` (String) Load balancing stickiness model. Valid values: none, autocookie, customcookie, iphash, least_conn.
- `transport_mode` (String) Transport protocol. Valid values: default, http, https, port_bridge.

### Optional

- `back_hosts` (Block Set) Set of backend hosts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--back_hosts))
- `description` (String) Description of the backend service.
- `mtls_certificate` (String) ID of mTLS certificate attached to backend service.
- `mtls_trusted_certificate` (String) ID of CA certificate attached to backend service.
- `sticky_cookie_name` (String) Custom cookie name for sticky sessions (used when sticky is 'customcookie').

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier for the backend service.

<a id="nestedblock--back_hosts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `back_hosts`

Required:

- `backup` (Boolean) Whether this is a backup host.
- `down` (Boolean) Whether the host is marked as down.
- `fail_timeout` (Number) Timeout in seconds after which failed attempts are reset.
- `host` (String) URI or IP address of the backend host.
- `http_ports` (List of Number) HTTP port numbers (e.g. [80, 8080]).
- `https_ports` (List of Number) HTTPS port numbers (e.g. [443, 8443]).
- `max_fails` (Number) Maximum number of failed attempts before marking host as down.
- `monitor_state` (String) Monitor state of the host.
- `weight` (Number) Weight for load balancing.
